title: Kali puja
intro: Kali Puja is one of the most important Hindu festivals celebrated mainly in West Bengal, Assam, Odisha, and other parts of Eastern India.
description:
Kali Puja is one of the most important Hindu festivals celebrated mainly in West Bengal, Assam, Odisha, and other parts of Eastern India. The festival is dedicated to Goddess Kali, the powerful and fierce form of Goddess Durga. Kali Puja is observed on the Amavasya (new moon night) of the Hindu month of Kartik, usually during October or November, on the same night when many parts of India celebrate Diwali.

According to Hindu traditions, Goddess Kali represents divine power, time, transformation, protection, and the destruction of evil forces. She is believed to have appeared to defeat negative energies and restore righteousness. Devotees worship Maa Kali to seek courage, protection, strength, and freedom from fear and negativity.

Kali Puja holds special importance in Bengal, where grand pandals are decorated, beautiful idols of Maa Kali are installed, and special prayers, Aarti, devotional songs, and rituals are performed throughout the night. Devotees offer flowers, sweets, lamps, and prayers to the Goddess. The festival symbolizes the victory of divine power over evil, light over darkness, and courage over fear.

Festival Month

Kartik (October – November)

Significance / Importance

Kali Puja has great religious and spiritual significance in Hinduism. Goddess Kali is worshipped as the embodiment of Shakti (divine feminine power), courage, protection, and transformation.

Devotees believe that worshipping Maa Kali removes fear, negativity, and obstacles while bringing strength, confidence, and spiritual protection. The festival reminds people that truth and righteousness always overcome darkness and evil.

Puja Vidhi

1. Clean and decorate the पूजा area and home.
2. Install an idol or image of Goddess Kali.
3. Light lamps and invoke Maa Kali with devotion.
4. Offer flowers, fruits, sweets, incense, and Naivedya.
5. Chant Kali Mantras and recite Kali Stotras.
6. Perform Maa Kali Aarti.
7. Distribute Prasad and pray for protection, strength, and prosperity.

Related Katha / Mantra / Aarti / Chalisa

* Maa Kali Katha
* Kali Mantra
* Kali Stotra
* Durga Saptashati
* Kali Aarti
* Durga Aarti

faq.a1: Kali Puja is celebrated on the Amavasya (new moon night) of the Hindu month of Kartik, usually in October or November.
faq.a2: Kali Puja is celebrated to worship Goddess Kali and seek her blessings for protection, strength, and victory over negativity.
faq.q3: Where is Kali Puja mainly celebrated?
faq.a3: Kali Puja is mainly celebrated in West Bengal, Assam, and Odisha.
faq.q4: What is the connection between Kali Puja and Diwali?
faq.a4: Kali Puja is celebrated on the same night as Diwali in many eastern states of India.
faq.a5: Goddess Kali is a powerful form of Goddess Durga and represents divine energy, protection, and destruction of evil.
faq.q6: Which rituals are performed during Kali Puja?
faq.a6: Devotees perform prayers, offer flowers, light lamps, chant Kali Mantras, and perform Aarti.
faq.q7: In which month is Kali Puja celebrated?
faq.a7: Kali Puja is usually celebrated in October or November.
faq.q8: What is the spiritual significance of Kali Puja?
faq.a8: Kali Puja symbolizes divine strength, courage, protection, transformation, and the victory of light over darkness.
